attractions.name: Barbara K. and W. Turrentine Jackson Hall
attractions.description: Jackson Hall is the premier 1,801-seat performance venue within the Robert and Margrit Mondavi Center for the Performing Arts at UC Davis. Designed with a 'box-within-a-box' architectural structure to isolate sound from nearby trains and highways, it is renowned for its world-class acoustics and stunning interior featuring floor-to-ceiling virgin Douglas fir paneling reclaimed from a Canadian lake (Mondavi Center, Visit California).

attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Tuesday–Friday, 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Will Call: Opens one hour prior to performances (Mondavi Center)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Friday (Ticket Office); Performance days vary by schedule (Mondavi Center)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; UC Davis students receive their first ticket free and 50% off thereafter; 25% discount for non-UC Davis students and UC Davis faculty/staff on subscriptions; $5 tickets available for students in qualifying courses; children under 18 receive 50% off (Mondavi Center, COSAF UC Davis)
highlights: '1,801-seat capacity across three levels: Orchestra, Grand Tier, and Upper Tier ([Event Tickets Center)', 'Interior paneling made from 500-year-old Douglas fir logs reclaimed from the bottom of Ruby Lake (Visit California)', 'State-of-the-art adjustable acoustics including a movable orchestra shell on air casters and a variable-height acoustical canopy (Arcca Digest)', 'Sustainable design featuring an energy-efficient displacement ventilation system and eco-friendly construction materials (Architizer)', "Atmospheric lighting including fiber-optic 'star lights' in the chandelier and balcony fronts (Arcca Digest)"]
attractions.tips: There is no formal dress code, but many guests choose to dress up for performances; classical music attendees are encouraged to applaud between complete works rather than movements; silence all digital devices and lower screen brightness if photography is permitted during curtain calls (Mondavi Center).

attractions.name: Bohart Museum of Entomology (Official Site)
attractions.description: The R. M. Bohart Museum of Entomology, founded in 1946, is a prominent research and educational facility located on the University of California, Davis campus. As the seventh largest insect collection in North America, it houses over seven million specimens of terrestrial and freshwater arthropods from around the globe. The museum is dedicated to understanding, documenting, and communicating biodiversity through its research collections, live "petting zoo," and public outreach programs (Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: Academic Surge Building, Room 1124, 455 Crocker Lane (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Mon: Closed to public (Research only); Tue: 1:00 pm - 4:30 pm (Walk-in); Wed: 1:00 pm - 4:30 pm (Walk-in); Thu: Closed to public (Pre-arranged tours only); Fri: Closed to public (Research only); Sat-Sun: Closed (Except for special events) (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday (Public walk-in); Thursday (Pre-arranged tours); Select Weekends (Open Houses) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free for individuals, families, and small informal groups; fee-based tours for large groups; free for UC Davis groups with pre-arrangement (Official Site)
highlights: 'Seventh largest insect collection in North America ([Official Site)', 'Live "petting zoo" featuring walking sticks and Madagascar hissing cockroaches (Bug Squad Blog)', 'Collection of over seven million specimens worldwide (Official Site)', 'Insect-themed gift shop with jewelry, books, and collecting equipment (Official Site)', 'Themed weekend open house events for the general public (Official Site)', 'Home to the California Insect Survey and diagnostic services (Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Check the official calendar for weekend open houses and known public closures during university breaks; park in Lot 46, which is free on weekends (Official Site).

attractions.name: California Raptor Center
attractions.description: The California Raptor Center (CRC) is an educational and research facility at UC Davis dedicated to the rehabilitation of injured and orphaned birds of prey. It serves as a home for non-releasable raptors that act as educational ambassadors, allowing visitors to see species like eagles, owls, and hawks up close. The center also provides hands-on training for students and conducts vital research on raptor health and conservation. California Raptor Center
attractions.hours: Visiting Hours: Monday-Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM. Office Hours: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM. California Raptor Center
attractions.admission: Self-guided tours: Free (for groups of 6 or fewer); Guided tours/On-site presentations: Small fee (scheduled in advance). California Raptor Center
highlights: ['Permanent collection of 30+ non-releasable raptor ambassadors including eagles, hawks, and owls.', 'On-site museum featuring taxidermied animals and interactive educational displays for all ages.', "Self-guided tour route with informative placards detailing each resident bird's personal story and species facts.", 'Unique rehabilitation program that treats 100-200 injured birds annually with a 60% release rate.', 'Affiliation with the top-ranked UC Davis School of Veterinary Medicine for advanced medical care.', 'Special annual events such as the Spring Open House featuring up-close bird presentations.']
attractions.tips: Keep self-guided tour groups to 6 people or fewer to minimize stress to the birds. Wear comfortable layers as most of the facility is outdoors. Bring water, especially during warmer months. Do not drive over the cattle grates when approaching the parking lot via the levee road. California Raptor Center, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Carolee Shields White Flower Garden and Gazebo UC Davis Arboretum
attractions.description: The Carolee Shields White Flower Garden and Gazebo is a theme garden based on medieval moon-viewing gardens of India and Japan. It features curving paths framing a vine-covered gazebo and is planted with fragrant, pale flowers that are particularly luminous by moonlight. The garden is named in honor of Carolee Shields, an avid gardener and the wife of Judge Peter J. Shields, one of the founders of the UC Davis campus. UC Davis Arboretum

attractions.tips: Visit during a full moon for the best experience as the white flowers reflect the moonlight; the garden is a quiet spot for picnics and is particularly fragrant in the evening. Parking is free on weekends and university holidays. Tripadvisor, UC Davis Arboretum

attractions.name: Community Park
attractions.description: Community Park is a premier 28-acre recreation hub in the heart of Davis, serving as a vibrant central gathering spot for residents and visitors alike. The park features an extensive array of amenities including the imaginative Rainbow City playground, a large pool complex, and diverse sports facilities. It is seamlessly connected to the city's famous greenbelt system, making it a popular destination for active local life and community events. (City of Davis, LocalWiki)
attractions.hours: Open 1/2 hour before sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset (City of Davis)
attractions.admission: Free for park entry; Pool entry: Ages 0-2: Free, Ages 3+: $7.00, Family Fun Night: $5.00 (City of Davis)
highlights: ['Rainbow City and Roundhouse playgrounds for diverse age groups', 'Davis Community Pool complex with seasonal recreational swimming', 'Skate park and a dedicated bike pump track', 'Lighted tennis courts, softball fields, and sand volleyball pits', 'Secure, fenced dog exercise area with separate zones', 'Large reservable group picnic areas equipped with BBQ grills']
attractions.tips: Plan your visit for the morning or late afternoon to avoid the intense California summer heat. The park is a top local spot for watching the annual July 4th fireworks display. Note that the pool complex is cash-free, and advance reservations are recommended for group picnic areas and athletic fields. (Yelp, City of Davis)

attractions.name: Downtown Davis
attractions.description: Downtown Davis is a vibrant 32-block Business Improvement District known for its walkable and bike-friendly streets, locally owned boutiques, and diverse culinary scene. As the heart of the city, it serves as a community hub for events, public art, and nightlife, offering a unique blend of college-town charm and cultural experiences. The area is home to numerous galleries and the world-renowned Davis Farmers Market in Central Park.
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Tuesday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Wednesday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Thursday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Friday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Saturday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary); Sunday: Open 24 hours (Business hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Davis Farmers Market: A nationally recognized year-round market held at Central Park every Saturday and Wednesday evening.', '2nd Friday ArtAbout: A monthly self-guided art walk featuring local exhibits, receptions, and live music across downtown businesses.', 'Public Art: Over 40 murals and sculptures integrated into the streetscape as part of the Davis Transmedia Art Walk.', 'Picnic in the Park: A seasonal Wednesday evening event (April–August) with live music, food vendors, and family activities.', "Bike-Friendly Culture: Extensive bicycle infrastructure and rental options, consistent with Davis's reputation as a top cycling city.", 'Aggie Deals: Special discounts and promotions tailored for UC Davis students at various downtown establishments.']
attractions.tips: Most public parking in the district is free for up to 3 hours, but be mindful of 're-parking' rules that prevent moving to a spot on the same block. For longer visits, the 4th & G Street Garage offers the first 3 hours free. Note that street parking is prohibited between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM on many blocks for maintenance. Exploring by bicycle is highly recommended due to the area's flat terrain and excellent bike paths.

attractions.name: Native American Contemplative Garden
attractions.description: The Native American Contemplative Garden is an outdoor reflective space dedicated to honoring the Patwin people, the original inhabitants of the land where UC Davis now sits. As part of a pioneering campus-wide project, the garden serves as both a memorial and an educational resource, featuring symbolic structures and native flora that highlight the resilience and heritage of the local Indigenous community. It offers a tranquil environment along the historic Putah Creek for visitors to reflect on the history and contributions of the Patwin people. UC Davis News, UC Davis Arboretum

attractions.name: Putah Creek Riparian Reserve
attractions.description: The UC Davis Putah Creek Riparian Reserve is a 640-acre natural riparian and grassland ecosystem that runs along the southern edge of the UC Davis campus. It is managed for wildlife protection, research, teaching, and passive recreation, offering a rare look at California's native riparian habitat. Visitors can enjoy activities such as hiking, bird watching, fishing, and seasonal swimming along the north side of the creek.

attractions.name: Slide Hill Park (Manor Park)
attractions.description: Slide Hill Park is a 12-acre community destination in East Davis famous for its iconic multi-level concrete sliding hill. The park features a wide array of amenities including the Manor Pool complex, sports courts, and renovated play structures, making it a "rite of passage" for local children. LocalWiki, MYDAVISCALIFORNIA
attractions.hours: Park: Monday-Sunday: Dawn to Dusk; Manor Pool (Summer): Mon-Fri: 1:00 p.m.–5:00 p.m., Mon/Wed Night: 7:15 p.m.–9:00 p.m., Sat-Sun: 1:00 p.m.–6:00 p.m. Airial Travel, City of Davis
attractions.admission: Park Entry: Free; Manor Pool: Ages 0-2 Free, Ages 3+ $7.00 per entry ($5.00 for Family Fun Night) City of Davis, Airial Travel
highlights: ['Iconic large concrete sliding hill (cardboard recommended)', 'Manor Pool complex with water slides, diving boards, and splash pad', 'Recently renovated playground structure (May 2019)', 'Fenced and unfenced dog exercise areas', 'Sports facilities including tennis, pickleball, basketball, and volleyball courts', 'Reservable group picnic areas with BBQ grills']
attractions.tips: Bring your own cardboard (BYOC) for the concrete slide, or use a rice sack for extra speed; wear shoes you don't mind scuffing. Parking in the main lot is limited and fills quickly during pool hours; street parking is available on Tulip Lane. Yelp, MYDAVISCALIFORNIA
attractions.nearby: Mace Ranch Park, Explorit Science Center, UC Davis Arboretum, Central Park, California Raptor Center Airial Travel, Homes.com

attractions.name: Social Sciences and Humanities Building (Death Star)
attractions.description: The Social Sciences and Humanities Building is a five-story, labyrinthine structure designed by architect Antoine Predock. It is famously nicknamed the "Death Star" by students due to its futuristic, metallic silver appearance and famously confusing interior layout. The building's design is intended to mimic the topography of the Sacramento Valley and the geological forces that formed the region.
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday: Closed (Building generally locks at 5 PM, though specific office hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Designed by renowned architect Antoine Predock in an "abstract landscape" style.', 'Nicknamed the "Death Star" for its metallic panels and maze-like hallways.', 'Houses multiple academic departments including Economics, Sociology, and History.', 'Features a 5th-floor tower with panoramic views of the UC Davis campus.', 'Includes an underground floor and multiple open-air courtyards.', 'Unconventional room numbering system that often confuses newcomers.']
attractions.tips: Use a building map or the campus wireless coverage map to help navigate the labyrinthine halls. Visit the 5th-floor library for one of the best elevated views in Davis. Be aware that the doors typically lock at 5 PM on weekdays.
attractions.nearby: Memorial Union (MU), UC Davis Arboretum, Shields Library, Young Hall

attractions.name: Sonoma Valley (Sonoma Wine Country)
attractions.description: Sonoma Valley is the premier destination for wine, food, arts, culture, and history in Northern California, known as the "Valley of the Moon" and the birthplace of the state's premium wine industry. Located less than an hour from Davis, it offers a laid-back wine country experience featuring world-class wineries, historic missions, and a charming central plaza. Sonoma Valley Visitors Bureau, UC Davis
attractions.address: 453 1st Street East, Sonoma, CA 95476 (Historic Plaza), 24724 Arnold Drive, Sonoma, CA 95476 (Jacuzzi Family Vineyards)
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Visitor Centers), Individual attraction hours vary (e.g., Wineries typically 10:00 AM – 4:30 PM) Sonoma Valley Visitors Bureau, Yelp
attractions.admission: Free (Visitor centers are free to enter; individual attraction prices vary, e.g., Mission San Francisco Solano: $3 for adults, $2 for youth) California State Parks, Sonoma Valley Visitors Bureau
highlights: 'Visit the historic Sonoma Plaza, an eight-acre central park surrounded by shops, galleries, and restaurants [Sonoma Valley', 'Explore Mission San Francisco Solano, the last of the California missions and part of Sonoma State Historic Park California State Parks', 'Taste award-winning wines at over 100 wineries, including historic estates like Buena Vista and Sebastiani Sonoma Valley', "Tour Jack London State Historic Park to see the ruins of the famous author's Wolf House and museum California State Parks", 'Enjoy farm-to-table dining at renowned restaurants like The Girl & the Fig or Café La Haye Sonoma Valley']
attractions.tips: Start your trip at the Sonoma Plaza for a mix of history, shopping, and tasting; book winery appointments in advance as many no longer accept walk-ins; and consider a self-guided vineyard walk at participating wineries for an up-close look at the vines. Sonoma Valley
attractions.nearby: Napa Valley, Jack London State Historic Park, Cornerstone Sonoma, Sonoma Raceway Sonoma Valley

attractions.name: Séka Hills Olive Mill and Tasting Room
attractions.category: Shopping (Olive Mill and Tasting Room) Tripadvisor
attractions.description: Owned and operated by the Yocha Dehe Wintun Nation, Séka Hills is a premier destination in the Capay Valley showcasing estate-grown olive oils, wines, and artisanal products. The Tasting Room is located within a 14,000-square-foot state-of-the-art olive mill facility, offering visitors an insider's view of the production process and scenic views of the surrounding blue hills. Seka Hills Brooks, Yocha Dehe
attractions.hours: Wednesday-Sunday 11:00am-5:00pm Seka Hills Contact
attractions.admission: Free (Olive Mill Tours are free of charge, no ticket needed; small fee for wine/olive oil tastings which may be refundable with purchase) Seka Hills Calendar, Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Guided tours and tastings of estate-grown olive oils and wines [Seka Hills Brooks', 'Artisan deli offering fresh seasonal salads, sandwiches, and charcuterie Seka Hills Brooks', '14,000 square foot state-of-the-art olive mill facility Seka Hills Brooks', "Scenic views of the Capay Valley and 'blue hills' (Séka) Seka Hills Brooks", 'Gift shop featuring pantry staples, cookbooks, and local gifts Seka Hills Brooks', 'Sustainable farming operation run by the Yocha Dehe Wintun Nation Seka Hills Story']
attractions.tips: Groups of ten or more should call ahead to arrange private tastings and tours. Visit during the fall (October-November) to witness the olive crush season. The deli closes at 4:00pm, an hour before the tasting room. Seka Hills Brooks, Yolo County Wines
attractions.nearby: Cache Creek Casino, Capay Valley Vineyards, Capay Valley Lavender, Capay Open Space Park, Full Belly Farm Tripadvisor

attractions.name: T. Elliot Weier Redwood Grove UC Davis Arboretum
attractions.category: Park, Landmark, Arboretum Collection UC Davis Arboretum
attractions.description: The T. Elliot Weier Redwood Grove is one of the largest collections of coast redwood trees (Sequoia sempervirens) outside their native range, providing a shady, silent, cathedral-like atmosphere just minutes from central campus. Named for Dr. T. Elliot Weier, a botany professor who helped establish the Arboretum in 1936, the grove also features giant sequoias, deciduous dawn redwoods, and a lush understory of native forest plants. UC Davis Arboretum, LocalWiki
attractions.address: Mrak Hall Dr (near Lot 5), Davis, CA 95616 UC Davis Arboretum
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day LocalWiki, MyDavisCalifornia
attractions.admission: Free UC Davis Arboretum
highlights: 'One of the largest coast redwood collections outside their native range [UC Davis Arboretum', 'Towering trees providing a "cathedral-like" atmosphere and cooling shade UC Davis Arboretum', 'Diverse species including coast redwoods, giant sequoias, and dawn redwoods LocalWiki', 'Renovated gathering circle with a small fountain LocalWiki', 'Shade-loving native understory plants like red osier dogwoods LocalWiki', 'Designated picnic areas with tables and benches AAA']
attractions.tips: Lot 5 is the closest visitor parking to the grove; parking is free on weekends and holidays but requires payment via the AggiePark app on weekdays. This is an ideal location to escape summer heat as the dense canopy keeps the area significantly cooler than the rest of the campus. UC Davis Arboretum, AAA
attractions.nearby: Mary Wattis Brown Garden of California Native Plants, Wyatt Deck, Lake Spafford, Putah Creek Lodge UC Davis Arboretum, Visit Yolo

attractions.name: The Davis Graduate ("The Grad") UC Davis Magazine
attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, Entertainment UC Davis Magazine
attractions.description: The Davis Graduate, affectionately known as "The Grad," was a long-standing sports bar and restaurant by day and a vibrant dance club by night. It served as a community hub for nearly five decades, hosting themed nights ranging from salsa and country line dancing to karaoke and World Cup viewing parties. UC Davis Magazine, The California Aggie

attractions.admission: Free (no cover) for those 21 & over before 9:00 PM; cover typically $5-$10 for evening events/dancing, with discounts for college students with ID LocalWiki, The California Aggie

attractions.name: The Eggheads (The Egghead Series) UC Davis
attractions.category: Landmark, Art Installation UC Davis, LocalWiki
attractions.description: The Eggheads are a collection of seven whimsical bronze sculptures across five locations on the UC Davis campus, created by artist and professor Robert Arneson. These iconic pieces use the 'egghead' motif to satirize and celebrate academia, knowledge, and the university experience. They have become a beloved symbol of campus life and a fixture for student traditions since their installation in the early 1990s. UC Davis, The Aggie
attractions.address: One Shields Avenue, Davis, CA 95616 UC Davis, LocalWiki
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public campus access) Airial Travel
attractions.admission: Free UC Davis, Airial Travel
highlights: 'Bookhead: Located in front of Shields Library, students touch it for good luck on exams. [UC Davis', 'Eye on Mrak (Fatal Laff): Features an upside-down laughing face and a watchful third eye aimed at the administration building. UC Davis', 'Stargazer: A contemplative sculpture looking straight up at the heavens, located near North Hall. UC Davis', 'Yin & Yang: Two heads in conversation near the Art/Music/Theatre complex. UC Davis', 'See No Evil/Hear No Evil: Two earless heads in the Mrak Hall roundabout, representing a commentary on communication. UC Davis']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes as the five locations are spread across the campus. Use an interactive campus map to find all seven heads. Visit during weekday mornings for better photo opportunities without crowds. Airial Travel, UC Davis
attractions.nearby: UC Davis Arboretum, Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum of Art, Shields Library, Mrak Hall UC Davis, Airial Travel

attractions.name: UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden
attractions.category: Museum / Park (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)
attractions.description: The UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden spans over 5,300 acres of the university campus, including a 100-acre historic arboretum with themed demonstration gardens and scientific collections. It serves as a "living laboratory" and outdoor museum, featuring the Putah Creek Riparian Reserve, a rare stream and grassland ecosystem managed for teaching, research, and wildlife protection (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden, Tripadvisor).
attractions.hours: Gardens and Landscapes: Open 24 hours daily. Headquarters Office: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, 365 days a year (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)
attractions.admission: Free (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)
highlights: '100-acre historic Arboretum featuring themed collections like the Redwood Grove and Acacia Grove ([UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)', 'The 3.5-mile loop walk around the Arboretum waterway, popular for walking and running (Tripadvisor)', 'Putah Creek Riparian Reserve offering opportunities for hiking, swimming, and boating (Visit Yolo)', 'Specialized gardens including the East Asian Collection, Mediterranean Collection, and California Native Plant Garden (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)', "Nature's Gallery Court, a public art project featuring 140 hand-painted ceramic tiles showcasing regional flora (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)", 'Arboretum Teaching Nursery, which hosts seasonal public plant sales for the community (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden)']
attractions.tips: Parking is free on weekends and holidays but requires a paid permit ($17/day or hourly) on weekdays via the AggiePark app. Dogs are welcome but must remain on a leash at all times (UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden). Comfortable walking shoes are recommended as the 3.5-mile loop covers significant ground (Visit Yolo).
attractions.nearby: Davis Downtown, Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum of Art, Robert and Margrit Mondavi Center for the Performing Arts (Davis Downtown, UC Davis)

attractions.name: UC Davis Quad
attractions.description: The Quad is the historic heart of the UC Davis campus and the primary center for student life and gathering. It features acres of expansive green lawn shaded by mature trees, providing a picturesque space for recreation, studying, and major university events. (UC Davis, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: One Shields Avenue (Centrally located between North, South, East, and West Quad Streets)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Campus buildings surrounding the Quad have individual hours) (Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel)
attractions.phone: (530) 752-1011 (General Campus) or (530) 754-0672 (Conference and Event Services) (UC Davis Admissions, UC Davis CES)
highlights: 'Central hub for student life and campus activities ([UC Davis)', 'Expansive green lawn ideal for picnics and relaxation (UC Davis)', 'Site of major events like Picnic Day and the Whole Earth Festival (UC Davis Admissions)', 'Surrounded by historic buildings like the Memorial Union and Hart Hall (Wikipedia)', 'Features mature shade trees and student-accessible hammocks (UC Davis Graduate Admissions)']
attractions.tips: Bring a blanket and enjoy a picnic or a nap under the large shade trees. Visit during the annual Picnic Day in April or the Whole Earth Festival in May for the most vibrant experience. The Quad is a great starting point for exploring the nearby UC Davis Arboretum or walking into downtown Davis. (Airial Travel, UC Davis Admissions)
attractions.nearby: UC Davis Arboretum, Peter J. Shields Library, Memorial Union, Manetti Shrem Museum of Art, Downtown Davis (UC Davis, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Yolo Fruit Stand (Visit Yolo)
attractions.description: Yolo Fruit Stand is a long-standing, family-run roadside market located just off I-80 near the Yolo Causeway. It specializes in a vast array of fresh seasonal produce, local nuts, honey, and international grocery items at bargain prices (Visit Yolo, DavisWiki). The stand is a popular stop for travelers and locals alike, offering unique treats like mint yogurt soda, freshly made fruit smoothies, and Gunther's ice cream (CBS Sacramento, DavisWiki).
attractions.hours: Spring/Summer: 8:00 am – 7:00 pm daily; Winter: 8:00 am – 6:00 pm daily (DavisWiki)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Yolo)
highlights: 'Wide variety of fresh, local seasonal produce at highly competitive prices ([Visit Yolo)', 'Extensive selection of nuts, dried fruits, and regional honey (DavisWiki)', 'International grocery aisle featuring Middle Eastern specialties and imported treats (CBS Sacramento)', 'On-site smoothie bar serving fresh watermelon, strawberry, and cantaloupe blends (DavisWiki)', "Retailer of famous Gunther's ice cream and fruit freezes (DavisWiki)", 'Convenient location for road-trip stops between Davis and Sacramento (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Try the watermelon smoothies in the summer and explore the unique international snacks; be sure to wash produce thoroughly as it comes directly from local farms (DavisWiki, CBS Sacramento).
attractions.nearby: Vic Fazio Yolo Wildlife Area, UC Davis Arboretum, and Yolo Causeway (Visit Yolo, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Bistro 33 Davis
attractions.description: Bistro 33 was a cultural and community hub located in the renovated Historic City Hall building in downtown Davis. It was renowned for its Pacific Northwest-inspired cuisine, signature wood-fired pizzas, and vibrant atmosphere, particularly its popular "Mojito Nights" on the expansive outdoor patio. (Yelp, LocalWiki)
attractions.hours: Sun: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Mon-Wed: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Thu-Sat: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM (LocalWiki)
attractions.admission: Free (LocalWiki)
highlights: 'Housed in the Spanish Colonial Revival-style Historic City Hall building ([Davis Downtown)', 'Famous three-tiered outdoor patio with a "burning rock" fireplace (Yelp)', 'Popular Thursday "Mojito Pitcher Nights," a local college town tradition (The California Aggie)', 'Menu featuring Pacific Northwest-inspired paninis, salads, and wood-fired pizzas (Bistro 33 On The Go)', 'Versatile space including a full-service bar, banquet hall, and regular dining seating (LocalWiki)']
attractions.tips: Make reservations for the patio during peak hours as it fills up quickly; visit on Thursday nights for mojito specials; note that the venue historically turned into a louder, club-like atmosphere after 9:00 PM. (LocalWiki, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Davis Commons, Central Park, UC Davis Arboretum, Varsity Theatre, US Bicycling Hall of Fame (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Buckhorn Steakhouse
attractions.description: Buckhorn Steakhouse is a family-owned icon located in the historic De Vilbiss Hotel building in downtown Winters. Known for its in-house butcher program and proprietary beef aging, it offers a welcoming atmosphere filled with local memorabilia and taxidermy trophies. Buckhorn Steakhouse Official Site

attractions.admission: Entrees range from approximately $28 to $70+ (e.g., Char-broiled steak ~$28, Ribeye ~$35-46, Tomahawk ~$70+). Tripadvisor, Reddit

attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for the dining room, while The DeVilbiss Room is first-come, first-served. Don't miss the holiday season when the taxidermy animals are specially decorated. Buckhorn Steakhouse Official Site, Yelp

Located in the heart of downtown Davis, it has become a staple for students and locals alike, offering a casual and lively atmosphere for enjoying over 25 different burger varieties and specialties. attractions.name: California State Capitol and Museum
attractions.description: The California State Capitol serves as the working seat of the California state government and a living museum. Modeled after the U.S. Capitol, the Neoclassical building houses the chambers of the California State Legislature and the Governor's office, set within the scenic 40-acre Capitol Park. (California State Capitol Museum, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (California State Capitol Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Friday (Building); Daily (Capitol Park) (California State Capitol Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (California State Capitol Museum, California State Assembly)
highlights: 'Historic legislative chambers for the State Assembly and Senate ([Tripadvisor)', 'Ornate interior rotunda with a decorated dome and historic architecture (California State Capitol Museum)', 'Portrait gallery of former California governors, including Jerry Brown and Arnold Schwarzenegger (Visit California)', '40-acre Capitol Park featuring memorials, monuments, and trees from around the world (California State Capitol Museum)', 'World Peace Rose Garden with over 650 varieties of roses (California Through My Lens)', "County dioramas in the West Wing hallway showcasing each of California's 58 counties (Visit California)"]
attractions.tips: Free public tours are available hourly on the weekday between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM; sign up in Room B-27 on the lower level. Visitors must pass through a security screening including metal detectors. While the building is closed on weekends, Capitol Park remains open for visitors to explore the grounds. (California State Capitol Museum, Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: Central Park is the heart of civic life in Davis, featuring expansive green spaces, a covered pavilion, and the Rotary Stage (City of Davis). It serves as a community hub for major events, most notably the world-renowned Davis Farmers Market which brings the community together year-round (Visit Yolo).

attractions.name: Grasslands Regional Park
attractions.description: Grasslands Regional Park is a 320-acre natural area featuring restored native grasslands, vernal pools, and habitat for rare species like Burrowing owls and Swainson's hawks. It offers a 1.25-mile trail loop and a unique 10-acre off-leash dog park within a natural grassland setting, while also hosting recreational clubs for archery and model airplane soaring.
attractions.hours: Daily from sunrise to sunset Yolo County Grasslands Regional Park, California Local
attractions.admission: Free for visitors using the trail and dog park; [1] $10.00 per vehicle for general park use (excluding specific trail/dog park areas where fees are often not enforced/collected for day use) Yolo County Parks Fee Information, Grasslands Regional Park Page

attractions.tips: Visitors should be cautious of foxtails, which are rampant in the park and can be harmful to dogs; check pets thoroughly after leaving. Note that potable water is not available on-site, and restrooms are limited to portable units provided by recreational clubs. The dog park is a natural field, not a manicured lawn, so expect uneven terrain and native vegetation.

Designed for cyclists of all ages and skill levels, the loop primarily utilizes off-road bike paths and quiet residential streets, offering a safe and scenic way to explore Davis. (City of Davis, Bike Davis)

attractions.name: Clarksburg Wine Country (Clarksburg AVA)
attractions.description: Clarksburg Wine Country is a premier American Viticultural Area (AVA) nestled along the Sacramento River Delta, known for its uncrowded, authentic vineyard experiences. The region benefits from a unique microclimate of warm days and cool 'Delta Breezes,' producing world-class Chenin Blanc and Petite Sirah across more than 35 different grape varietals. Visitors can explore a mix of historic landmarks, family-farmed estates, and the iconic Old Sugar Mill hub. (Enjoy Clarksburg, Clarksburg Winery Collective)
attractions.address: 35265 Willow Ave, Clarksburg, CA 95612 (Old Sugar Mill) / P.O. Box 308, Clarksburg, CA 95612 (CWGVA)
attractions.hours: Old Sugar Mill: Daily 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Individual winery hours vary; many open Friday-Sunday) (Old Sugar Mill); Heringer Estates: Mon-Fri 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sat-Sun 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Heringer Estates); Bogle Family Vineyards: Daily 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Bogle Winery)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Daily) (Old Sugar Mill, Heringer Estates)
attractions.admission: Free entry to region and Old Sugar Mill; Wine tasting fees range from $10 - $30 per person (often waived with bottle purchase) (Enjoy Clarksburg)
highlights: 'Home to the historic Old Sugar Mill, a 1934 sugar beet refinery housing 14+ boutique wineries ([Old Sugar Mill)', 'Specializes in unique varietals like Chenin Blanc and Petite Sirah influenced by Delta silt soils (Clarksburg Winery Collective)', 'Offers a scenic, uncrowded alternative to Napa and Sonoma with authentic family-farmed atmosphere (Visit Yolo)', 'Host to seasonal events including live music, food truck weekends, and the annual Grape Stomp (Enjoy Clarksburg)', 'Scenic riverfront views with historic metal truss bridges and levee-side drives (Visit Yolo)']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for weekend wine tastings to ensure availability. Check the food truck schedule at the Old Sugar Mill for Saturday and Sunday dining options. Bring a light jacket even on warm days, as the 'Delta Breeze' can significantly cool the area in the late afternoon. (Old Sugar Mill, Enjoy Clarksburg)
attractions.nearby: Sacramento River Cruise, Husick’s Taphouse, Dinky Diner, UC Davis Arboretum (Davis), Old Sacramento Waterfront (Visit Yolo, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Downtown Winters Historic District
attractions.description: Founded in 1875, the Downtown Winters Historic District is a charming, walkable area known for its well-preserved Victorian architecture and vibrant farm-to-table culinary scene. The district features a collection of historic buildings, including the 1899 DeVilbiss Hotel and the Winters Opera House, which now house award-winning restaurants, wineries, and boutique shops. It serves as a cultural hub for the region, hosting numerous community events, festivals, and live music throughout the year.
attractions.address: Main Street (between Railroad Avenue and First Street), Winters, CA 95694
attractions.hours: Historic District: Always accessible. Winters Visitor Center: Thursday 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM, Friday & Saturday 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sunday 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM. (General downtown business hours: Monday - Friday 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM) (Discover Winters)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Individual business hours vary; Visitor Center open Thursday-Sunday) (Discover Winters)
attractions.admission: Free (individual attractions like the Winters Museum are also free, while events and shows at the Winters Opera House require tickets) (Discover Winters, Historical Society of Winters)
highlights: ['Historic Victorian architecture and buildings dating back to the late 1800s.', 'Award-winning farm-to-fork dining at renowned spots like Buckhorn Steakhouse and Putah Creek Café.', 'Numerous boutique wine tasting rooms including Berryessa Gap Vineyards and Turkovich Family Wines.', 'Cultural venues like the Winters Opera House and the Palms Playhouse.', 'Vibrant community events such as the Fourth Friday Block Party and the annual Holiday Tractor Parade.', 'Scenic proximity to Putah Creek and its nature park.']
attractions.tips: Visit on the fourth Friday of the month for the Downtown Block Party featuring live music and local food. The town is very bike-friendly, so consider exploring on two wheels like many locals. Be sure to check event calendars for the famous annual Holiday Tractor Parade in December. (Visit Yolo, Discover Winters)
attractions.nearby: Putah Creek Nature Park, Lake Berryessa, Stebbins Cold Canyon Natural Reserve, Monticello Dam, Park Winters Flower Farm, and Berryessa Brewing Co. (Discover Winters, Visit Yolo)

attractions.name: Downtown Woodland Historic District
attractions.description: The Downtown Woodland Historic District is a California Historical Landmark and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places, encompassing roughly 370 acres and dozens of contributing buildings. Known as a 'museum of housing styles,' the area features a wealth of beautifully restored architecture from the 1860s to the 1940s, serving as the cultural and commercial heart of the city. Wikipedia, City of Woodland
attractions.address: Main Street (from Elm Street to Third Street)
attractions.hours: Generally accessible 24/7 as a public district; individual business hours typically Monday-Saturday 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sunday 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM Visit Woodland
attractions.admission: Free Stroll Through History
highlights: ['Woodland Opera House State Historic Park', 'Historic Hotel Woodland and Porter Building', 'Woodland Public Library (the oldest in California in continuous use)', 'Over 400 buildings showcasing classic American architectural styles', 'Monthly First Friday Art Walks with live music and food', "Self-guided 'Explore Historic Woodland' walking tours"]
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for the 7.5 miles of available walking tours; visit during 'First Friday' for special evening events; purchase the official walking tour booklet at City Hall for detailed history. City of Woodland
attractions.nearby: Heidrick Ag History Center, Yolo County Fairgrounds, Reiff’s Gas Station Museum Visit Woodland

attractions.description: Great Bear Vineyards is a boutique, family-run winery nestled on a historic 1860s estate on the northern edge of Davis, California. The vineyard produces award-winning, sustainably grown wines and offers a rustic setting featuring a restored 1860s barn, Spanish-style winery, and vibrant lavender fields. Official Website, UC Davis Magazine

attractions.name: Hooby's Brewing Farm
attractions.description: Hooby's Brewing Farm is a family-owned and operated seasonal brewery and working farm located on 40 acres just outside of Davis, CA. Visitors can enjoy craft beers in a rustic environment, roam the fields, interact with farm animals like goats and sheep, and watch planes from the nearby Yolo County Airport. (Hooby's Brewing, Visit Davis)
attractions.hours: Friday: 5:00 PM – 9:30 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM – 9:30 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Monday–Thursday: Closed (Seasonal hours vary; check official website for updates) (Hooby's Brewing)
attractions.admission: Free (Discover Winters)
highlights: "40-acre working farm with sheep, goats, chickens, and a pig ([Hooby's Brewing)", "Family and pet-friendly atmosphere with plenty of space to roam (Hooby's Brewing)", "Live music performances and seasonal events like line dancing (Hooby's Brewing)", "Picnic-friendly policy allowing outside food, with wood-fired pizza often available on-site (Hooby's Brewing)", 'Scenic views of the Yolo countryside and nearby airport activity (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Dress for a working farm environment as there are dirt and potholes; dogs are welcome but must remain on a leash at all times; no outside alcohol is permitted as only Hooby’s beer is served on-site. (Hooby's Brewing)
attractions.nearby: Yolo County Airport, Great Bear Vineyards, UC Davis Arboretum, Historic Downtown Winters (Yelp, Tripadvisor)

The estate serves as an award-winning wedding and event venue while offering unique day-visit experiences such as seasonal brunch, flower picking, and guided garden tours. Park Winters

attractions.name: River Fox Train
attractions.description: The River Fox Train provides a variety of themed excursion experiences along a historic 10-mile track through the scenic Yolo countryside. Passengers can choose from relaxing beer and wine trains, interactive murder mystery dinners, and unique railbike adventures where they pedal along the tracks. The journey offers a nostalgic look at California's agricultural heritage, passing through fields of sunflowers, wheat, and orchards. (Visit California, Visit Sacramento)
attractions.hours: Departure times vary by excursion. Ticketing and office support generally available 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Trains typically depart at scheduled times on weekends (e.g., 10:00 AM, 1:00 PM, 5:00 PM) and select weekdays for Railbikes. (Birdeye, River Fox Train)
attractions.days_open: Year-round; typically operates Friday through Sunday for trains, with Railbike excursions available on weekdays and weekends depending on the season. (River Fox Train)
attractions.admission: Railbikes: From $68.90, Yolo Explorer: From $15.60, Old Vine Express: From $47.40, Boba Tea Train: From $42.10, Afternoon Tea: From $95.05, Beer Train: From $105.70, Murder Mystery Dinner Train: From $205.34, Holiday/Seasonal Trains: From $105.70. Prices vary by excursion and tier (e.g., Golden Fox vs. Copper Fox). (River Fox Train Booking)
highlights: ['Unique Railbike excursions allowing guests to pedal custom-made bikes along the rails.', "Themed 'Refresher' trains including Beer, Wine, Mimosas, and Boba Tea experiences.", 'Interactive Murder Mystery Dinner shows with a three-course meal and live actors.', 'Seasonal holiday events such as the Magical Christmas Train and Pumpkin Patch Express.', 'Historic rail route dating back to 1911 through the agricultural heart of Yolo County.', 'Access to a private park at Elkhorn Station for certain spring and summer excursions.']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes prior to departure as boarding begins early and there are no refunds for missed rides. Wear comfortable, closed-toed shoes (flats or chunky heels recommended) due to gravel walkways and moving rail cars. Dress in layers to accommodate the delta breeze and potential dust in open-air cars. Note that the historic train cars are not wheelchair accessible and outside food/alcohol is strictly prohibited. (River Fox Train FAQ, Visit Sacramento)
attractions.nearby: Old Sacramento Waterfront, California State Railroad Museum, Sacramento Zoo, California State Capitol Park, Elkhorn Park. (Tripadvisor, UC Davis)

The facility features a newly renovated restaurant with original copper kettles at its heart and an expansive 3,500-square-foot beer garden designed for year-round enjoyment. Sudwerk Brewing Co., Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Mon-Tue: Closed; Wed-Fri: 4:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Sat: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Sun: 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM (Kitchen closes at 8:00 PM Wed-Sat) Sudwerk Brewing Co.

highlights: "Award-winning German-style lagers, including GABF 'Brewery of the Year' in 2021 [Sudwerk Brewing Co.", 'Expansive 3,500-square-foot beer garden with misters and fire pits Sudwerk Brewing Co.', 'Unique dining experience featuring original copper brewing kettles in the restaurant Sudwerk Brewing Co.', 'Weekly Sudwerk Pub Quiz held every Wednesday night Sudwerk Brewing Co.', "Collaborations with UC Davis, including California's first collegiate licensed beer Sudwerk Brewing Co.", 'Small arcade area featuring various pinball machines Yelp']

attractions.name: The Palms Playhouse
attractions.description: The Palms Playhouse is a legendary live music venue that originated in a rustic barn in South Davis in 1975. Now located in the historic Winters Opera House, it is renowned for its intimate "listening room" atmosphere and for hosting world-class performers in genres such as folk, Americana, blues, and bluegrass.
attractions.hours: Varies by performance; doors and box office typically open one hour before showtime (e.g., 7:00 PM for an 8:00 PM show).
attractions.admission: $20 - $55 (Varies by artist; check official website for specific show pricing)
highlights: ['Located in the historic Winters Opera House, dating back to 1875.', 'Originally founded in a rustic 19th-century barn in South Davis.', 'Renowned for exceptional acoustics and an intimate setting.', 'Hosts a diverse lineup of national and international touring acts.', 'Community-supported venue with a dedicated local following.', 'Features a hardwood dance floor and a bar serving regional beer and wine.']
attractions.tips: Seating is typically general admission, so arrive early for the best view. Check the online calendar in advance as many shows sell out quickly. Explore the charming shops and dining options in downtown Winters before the performance.
attractions.nearby: Berryessa Brewing Co., Turkovich Family Wines, Putah Creek, UC Davis Arboretum

attractions.name: University of California, Davis
attractions.description: The University of California, Davis (UC Davis) is a prestigious public land-grant research university and a "Public Ivy" located in Northern California. Originally founded in 1905 as an agricultural branch of the University of California, it has grown into a world-class institution renowned for its School of Veterinary Medicine and programs in agriculture, environmental science, and biological sciences. The campus is noted for its sustainability, bicycle-friendly culture, and extensive green spaces like the UC Davis Arboretum. Wikipedia, UC Davis About
attractions.hours: Arboretum: Open 24/7; Manetti Shrem Museum: Wed-Sat 10 AM - 5 PM; Administrative Offices: Mon-Fri 8 AM - 4 PM UC Davis Arboretum, Manetti Shrem Museum, UC Davis HQ Hours
attractions.admission: Free for campus attractions like the Arboretum and Manetti Shrem Museum; Parking is $2.75/hour or $17.00/day on weekdays Manetti Shrem Museum FAQ, UC Davis Transportation Services
highlights: 'UC Davis Arboretum and Public Garden with over 22,000 trees and plants [UC Davis Arboretum', 'Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum of Art featuring free contemporary exhibitions Manetti Shrem Museum', 'World-renowned School of Veterinary Medicine, the largest in the United States Wikipedia', 'The iconic "Egghead" sculptures by artist Robert Arneson located throughout the campus UC Davis Visit', 'Gold-level Bicycle Friendly University with extensive bike paths and infrastructure Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Parking is free on weekends and university holidays (except during special events); use the AggiePark app for convenient hourly parking payments on weekdays. Manetti Shrem Museum FAQ, UC Davis Transportation Services
attractions.nearby: Davis Greenbelt, California State Capitol (Sacramento), Sacramento Zoo, Old Sacramento Waterfront, Napa Valley Tripadvisor, UC Davis Travel

attractions.name: Yolo Bypass Wildlife Area (Vic Fazio Yolo Wildlife Area)
attractions.description: The Yolo Bypass Wildlife Area is a 16,600-acre restored wetland and upland habitat located in the heart of the Pacific Flyway. It serves as a vital sanctuary for over 200 species of birds, as well as fish, bats, and other Sacramento Valley wildlife. Visitors can explore the area through a six-mile auto tour loop or various seasonal walking trails. CDFW, Visit Yolo
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset, Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset, Friday: Sunrise - Sunset, Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset, Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset (Closed Christmas Day) CDFW
attractions.admission: Free for general wildlife viewing and birdwatching; Hunting requires a Type A hunting pass; Monthly guided tours typically suggest a $10 donation CDFW, Yolo Basin Foundation
highlights: 'Over 200 species of birds, including waterfowl, raptors, and shorebirds [Yolo Bird Alliance', 'Six-mile auto tour loop for convenient vehicle-based wildlife viewing Visit Yolo', 'Seasonal walking trails ranging from 0.78 to 3.3 miles Visit Yolo', 'Home to approximately 250,000 Mexican free-tailed bats during summer months Visit California', 'Monthly guided wetland tours offered from October through June Yolo Basin Foundation', 'Part of the Global-Priority Level Important Bird Area Sacramento Audubon']
attractions.tips: Always check for emergency closures due to flooding during winter months. Bring binoculars and a field guide for the best birdwatching experience. Dogs, bicycles, and horses are generally not allowed on the wildlife area to protect the habitat. Staying inside your vehicle on the auto loop often allows for closer views of birds. CDFW, Birdingplaces
attractions.nearby: UC Davis Arboretum, Davis Wetlands, Grasslands Regional Park, Sacramento Bypass Wildlife Area Visit Yolo

attractions.name: Lake Berryessa
attractions.description: Lake Berryessa is the largest lake in Napa County, California, and one of the largest bodies of freshwater in the state. Formed by the Monticello Dam on Putah Creek in the 1950s, it serves as a vital reservoir for irrigation and municipal water while offering extensive year-round recreation. The lake is a popular destination for boating, fishing, and hiking, nestled between the Blue Ridge and Cedar Roughs in the Vaca Mountains (Wikipedia, Bureau of Reclamation).
attractions.hours: Oak Shores Day Use: Monday–Friday 7:30 AM–5:00 PM, Saturday–Sunday 7:30 AM–7:00 PM (Summer hours extend to 8:00 PM); Administrative Office: Monday–Friday 8:00 AM–4:30 PM (Bureau of Reclamation, Recreation.gov)
attractions.admission: Free for general entry to Bureau of Reclamation day-use areas (e.g., Oak Shores); $10.00 for vehicle day-use pass via Recreation.gov; $10.00–$25.00 for boat launching depending on location and season (Recreation.gov, Bureau of Reclamation)
highlights: 'Home to the world-famous "Morning Glory Hole" (Glory Hole Spillway) at Monticello Dam ([SF Chronicle)', 'Extensive 165-mile shoreline with numerous quiet coves for exploration (NapaValley.com)', 'Prime destination for water sports, including houseboating, jet skiing, and kayaking (Visit Napa Valley)', 'Rich biodiversity with sightings of bald eagles, ospreys, and black-tailed deer (Foresyte Travel)', 'Excellent fishing for both cold
and warm-water species like trout, bass, and catfish (Cedar Gables Inn)', 'Scenic hiking trails such as the Smittle Creek Trail and North Shore Trail (Islands)']
attractions.tips: Visit on weekdays to avoid large crowds, especially during peak summer months between Memorial Day and Labor Day. Always check boat launch status and ensure watercraft are "clean, drain, and dry" to follow mandatory invasive species prevention protocols. Bring sun protection and plenty of water as the surrounding hills can reach high temperatures (Bureau of Reclamation, Foresyte Travel).
attractions.nearby: Monticello Dam, Putah Creek Wildlife Area, Berryessa Brewing Company (Winters), Napa Valley Wineries, Town of Winters (Tripadvisor, Visit Yolo)
